description: The yen (symbol: ¥; currency code: JPY) is the official currency of Japan. It is the third most-traded currency in the foreign exchange market after the United States dollar and the euro
phrase_type_id: and is widely used as a reserve currency. The yen is divided into 100 sen
view_id: though sen are no longer used in everyday transactions.

description: Pound sterling (symbol: £; currency code: GBP) is the official currency of the United Kingdom and several British overseas territories and Crown Dependencies. It is the oldest currency still in continuous use and the fourth most-traded currency on the foreign exchange market. One pound is divided into 100 pence.
The renminbi (symbol: ¥; currency code: CNY; abbreviation: RMB) is the official currency of the People's Republic of China. Its primary unit is the yuan. Issued by the People's Bank of China
phrase_type_id: the renminbi is one of the world's most-traded currencies and is included in the IMF's Special Drawing Rights basket.

description: The Australian dollar (symbol: $ or A$; currency code: AUD) is the official currency of the Commonwealth of Australia
phrase_type_id: including its external territories
view_id: as well as the independent Pacific states of Kiribati
impact: and Tuvalu. It is divided into 100 cents and was introduced in 1966

description: The Canadian dollar (symbol: $ or C$; currency code: CAD) is the official currency of Canada. It is issued by the Bank of Canada and is one of the world's most-traded currencies. The Canadian dollar is divided into 100 cents and is often regarded as a commodity currency due to Canada's substantial natural-resource exports.
The Swiss franc (symbol: Fr. or CHF; currency code: CHF) is the official currency and legal tender of Switzerland and Liechtenstein
phrase_type_id: and is also used in the Italian exclave of Campione d'Italia. Issued by the Swiss National Bank
view_id: it is widely regarded as a safe-haven currency due to Switzerland's political stability and low inflation.

description: A spot transaction is a foreign exchange deal in which two parties agree to exchange currencies at the current market rate
phrase_type_id: with settlement typically occurring within two business days.

description: A completed agreement between a buyer and a seller to exchange goods
phrase_type_id: services
view_id: or financial instruments. In foreign exchange
usage: a transaction typically involves the exchange of one currency for another at an agreed rate.

description: An outright forward is a foreign exchange contract obliging two parties to exchange currencies at a specified rate on a future date beyond the spot settlement window. It differs from a swap in that it consists of a single leg rather than two.

Forward contracts are agreements to buy or sell an asset at a predetermined price on a specified future date. Currency forwards are widely used to hedge foreign exchange risk arising from future cash flows in foreign currencies.

A currency swap (or FX swap) is a foreign exchange transaction that combines a spot purchase or sale of a currency with a simultaneous forward sale or purchase of the same currency. It is widely used to manage liquidity and hedge foreign exchange exposure.
A foreign exchange option gives the holder the right
phrase_type_id: but not the obligation
view_id: to exchange one currency for another at a specified rate on or before a specified date. Options are typically used to hedge or to speculate on currency movements.
